globalNews.title: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
globalNews.summary: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

globalNews.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
globalNews.summary: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

globalNews.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
globalNews.summary: Five people are killed and at least 35 injured as Ukraine's two largest cities are targeted, as Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

globalNews.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
globalNews.summary: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.

globalNews.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
globalNews.summary: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

cryptoNews.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
cryptoNews.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

cryptoNews.title: US agencies miss GENIUS Act deadline for final stablecoin rules
cryptoNews.summary: US regulators failed to finalize implementing regulations by the GENIUS Act’s one-year deadline, issuing 10 proposed rules instead.

cryptoNews.title: Upbit parent Dunamu faces South Korean regulator sanctions process: Report
cryptoNews.summary: Korea’s Virtual Asset User Protection Act lacks explicit sanctions provisions for hacking and similar incidents, clouding the scope of penalties facing the Upbit operator Dunamu.

cryptoNews.title: Electronic Transactions Association CEO Expecting More Partnerships with Bitcoin Startups
cryptoNews.summary: Electronic Transactions Association (ETA) CEO, Jason Oxman, indicated that members of his organization might start recognizing Bitcoin’s disruptive potential, suggesting that this might lead to more p

cryptoNews.title: Kaspersky identifies malware framework targeting crypto investors
cryptoNews.summary: Cybersecurity company Kaspersky said a newly identified malware framework is targeting cryptocurrency investors through social engineering tactics and trojanized GitHub apps.

aiNews.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
aiNews.summary: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

aiNews.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
aiNews.summary: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

aiNews.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
aiNews.summary: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

aiNews.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
aiNews.summary: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

aiNews.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
aiNews.summary: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.
